title: cephalotaxus harringtonia subsp. drupacea
intro: Cephalotaxus harringtonia subsp. drupacea, also known as the Chinese plum yew, is a plant species in the family Taxaceae that is native to southern China and northern Vietnam. The plant contains taxine, a toxic compound with medicinal properties, which has been used to treat various health issues. Research has shown that the plant may have health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and anti-cancer properties, although more studies are needed to confirm its efficacy. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems.
